title: vicia vicina
intro: Vicia vicina, also known as narrow-leaved vetch or narrow-leaved pea, is a type of perennial legume native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Vicia vicina can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its dried flowers and leaves can be used in tinctures and capsules.
